ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin (Agenda) Safe Mode reboot to disarm EDR before ransomware encryption
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Qilin/Agenda affiliate playbook of forcing a Windows reboot into Safe Mode (with networking) via bcdedit so endpoint protection drivers don't load, then detonating the Rust/Go ransomware binary. Microsoft tracks this cluster overlapping Storm-2575 / Agenda; Trend Micro and CrowdStrike have repeatedly documented this exact bcdedit→shutdown→encrypt chain.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=bcdedit.exe AND Processes.process IN ("*safeboot*network*","*safeboot*minimal*","*/set*safeboot*")) OR (Processes.process_name IN (vssadmin.exe,wmic.exe) AND Processes.process IN ("*delete*shadows*","*shadowcopy*delete*")) OR (Processes.process_name=wevtutil.exe AND Processes.process="*cl*") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | stats dc(process_name) as distinct_actions values(process) as cmds values(parent) as parents min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by dest user | where distinct_actions>=2 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql: let safeboot = DeviceProcessEvents | where FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe" | where ProcessCommandLine has "safeboot" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/set","-set") | project SafeTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName=AccountUpn, SafeCmd=ProcessCommandLine, SafeParent=InitiatingProcessFileName; let reboot = DeviceProcessEvents | where (FileName =~ "shutdown.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/r","-r")) or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "reboot") | project RebootTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RebootCmd=ProcessCommandLine; let killrecov = DeviceProcessEvents | where (FileName in~ ("vssadmin.exe","wmic.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("delete shadows","shadowcopy delete")) or (FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "delete catalog") or (FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("recoveryenabled no","bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures")) | project RecovTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RecovCmd=ProcessCommandLine; safeboot | join kind=inner (reboot) on DeviceId | where datetime_diff('minute', RebootTime, SafeTime) between (-30 .. 30) | join kind=leftouter (killrecov) on DeviceId | project SafeTime, DeviceName, AccountName, SafeParent, SafeCmd, RebootCmd, RecovCmd
ucs.rationale: bcdedit /set safeboot is essentially never used by legitimate admins on production endpoints, and Qilin/Agenda is the dominant ransomware family that pivots into Safe Mode-with-Networking specifically to neuter EDR minifilters before launching its Rust/Go encryptor. Pairing the bcdedit edit with a forced reboot inside a 30-minute window and shadow-copy/recovery destruction yields a Qilin-shaped ch

ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin mass browser-credential harvesting via GPO-deployed PowerShell on domain endpoints
ucs.description: Hunts the August-2024 Sophos-documented Qilin tradecraft where the affiliate, after privileged AD access, pushed a PowerShell logon script through Group Policy that copied Chrome/Edge 'Login Data', 'Cookies', and 'Local State' from every user profile on every domain-joined host before the ransomware stage. This pre-encryption credential sweep is a Qilin-distinctive behavior not seen in most RaaS playbooks.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN (powershell.exe,pwsh.exe,cmd.exe) AND (Processes.process="*\\User Data\\*Login Data*" OR Processes.process="*\\User Data\\*Cookies*" OR Processes.process="*\\User Data\\Local State*" OR Processes.process="*Chrome\\User Data*" OR Processes.process="*Edge\\User Data*") AND Processes.parent_process_name IN (gpscript.exe,userinit.exe,services.exe,svchost.exe,wscript.exe) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | stats dc(dest) as host_count values(cmdline) as cmds values(user) as users by parent_process_name | where host_count>=3 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql: let browser_artifacts = pack_array(@"\Google\Chrome\User Data\", @"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\", @"\BraveSoftware\Brave-Browser\User Data\", @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\"); let suspicious = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("gpscript.exe","userinit.exe","svchost.exe","services.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe") or InitiatingProcessParentFileName =~ "gpscript.exe" | where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe") | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Login Data","Cookies","Local State","Web Data",@"User Data") | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (browser_artifacts) or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Chrome","Edge","Brave","Firefox"); let file_touch = DeviceFileEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where FileName in~ ("Login Data","Cookies","Local State","Web Data") | where FolderPath has_any (browser_artifacts) | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","brave.exe","firefox.exe","OneDrive.exe","MsMpEng.exe","explorer.exe") | summarize FileEvents=count(), Files=make_set(FileName,10), Folders=make_set(FolderPath,10) by DeviceId, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine; suspicious | join kind=inner file_touch on DeviceId | summarize Hosts=dcount(DeviceId), SampleHosts=make_set(DeviceName,10), SampleCmd=any(ProcessCommandLine) by InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine | where Hosts >= 3
ucs.rationale: Sophos X-Ops attributed this exact GPO-pushed PowerShell logon script that drags Chrome/Edge 'Login Data' and 'Cookies' off thousands of endpoints to Qilin in 2024 — the fan-out from a single GPO parent (gpscript.exe → powershell.exe touching browser SQLite stores across many hosts simultaneously) is the discriminator. Generic infostealers (RedLine, Lumma) hit one host at a time; LockBit/BlackCat
